name: Neighborhood Housing Services Of Brooklyn Cdc, Inc.
description_short: HUD-certified nonprofit housing counseling agency in Brooklyn providing free homebuying education, tenant services, foreclosure intervention, and financial coaching to underserved communities.
description_long:
Neighborhood Housing Services of Brooklyn (NHS Brooklyn) is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization established to create and preserve affordable housing in underserved Brooklyn neighborhoods. The organization operates three physical offices (Flatbush, Canarsie, and Coney Island) and is HUD-certified, meaning its counselors meet federal standards for housing education and guidance.

NHS Brooklyn offers a comprehensive suite of free and low-cost services across multiple housing and financial domains. For homebuyers, they provide first-time homebuyer seminars, mortgage options counseling, and financial coaching. For current homeowners, they offer foreclosure intervention, home repair assistance, maintenance training, and insurance guidance. Tenant services include counseling on eviction defense, tenant rights, affordable housing lottery applications, succession rights, preferential rent claims, and rent freeze programs for seniors and people with disabilities. They also provide landlord training and property management support.

The organization distinguishes itself through HUD certification (a credential requiring specific training and oversight), multi-language accessibility through three neighborhood locations, and a mission explicitly rooted in community leadership and partnership with government and business. Their event calendar demonstrates active community engagement with regular webinars and in-person seminars on housing lotteries, homebuying, and financial topics.

As a nonprofit counseling agency, NHS Brooklyn's primary strength is providing unbiased, credential-backed guidance rather than selling financial products. The main caveat is that they provide counseling and referrals rather than direct financial assistance (except home repair help), and service availability may be limited by appointment capacity. Clients should expect to provide extensive documentation for intake purposes.
pros: HUD-certified housing counselors meeting federal training standards
Three physical office locations across Brooklyn neighborhoods (Flatbush, Canarsie, Coney Island)
Specialized tenant services including eviction defense counseling and rent freeze program assistance for seniors and disabled persons
Free financial coaching and homebuyer seminars (both webinar and in-person formats)
Foreclosure intervention and loss mitigation counseling
Guidance on affordable housing lotteries and Housing Connect applications
Home repair assistance program beyond counseling-only services
cons: Services primarily limited to Brooklyn residents; geographic service boundary may exclude nearby areas
Appointment-based model may create wait times during high-demand periods
Provides counseling and referrals but does not directly offer mortgage products, loans, or housing assistance funds
Requires extensive documentation (2+ years tax returns, recent bank statements, pay stubs, lease agreements) for intake
No indication of evening/weekend availability at all three locations or multilingual staff
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Brooklyn seeking unbiased mortgage education and pre-purchase counseling
Tenants facing eviction, seeking affordable housing, or needing rent freeze program applications
Homeowners in foreclosure or mortgage distress seeking intervention counseling
diagnosis: NHS Brooklyn is best for Brooklyn residents seeking free, unbiased housing counseling from HUD-certified advisors—whether as first-time homebuyers, tenants facing eviction, or homeowners in distress. The main caveat is that they provide guidance and referrals rather than direct financial products or housing funds, and services are geographically limited to Brooklyn.
services: Tenant rights counseling and eviction defense
Rent freeze program support (seniors and disabled persons)
Referrals to legal assistance and other social services
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
